concepts.title: Functions of a Stock Exchange
concepts.description: A stock exchange facilitates the buying and selling of securities between buyers and sellers. It provides a platform for price discovery, facilitates the transfer of securities, and acts as a medium for corporate financing.

concepts.title: Types of Financial Markets
concepts.description: Financial markets can be broadly classified into primary markets and secondary markets. Primary markets deal with the issuance of new securities, while secondary markets facilitate the buying and selling of existing securities.

concepts.title: Key Features of a Well-Developed Stock Market
concepts.description: A well-developed stock market has a significant number of listed companies, a high level of liquidity, and a well-established regulatory framework. It also provides a wide range of investment options and facilitates price discovery.

concepts.title: Mnemonics for Types of Financial Markets
concepts.description: To remember the types of financial markets, use the mnemonic 'PS' which stands for Primary and Secondary markets.

concepts.title: Difference between Primary and Secondary Markets
concepts.description: Students often confuse the difference between primary and secondary markets. Primary markets deal with the issuance of new securities, while secondary markets facilitate the buying and selling of existing securities. To avoid this, remember that primary markets are for new issues, while secondary markets are for existing ones.

concepts.title: Regulatory Framework of a Stock Market
concepts.description: The regulatory framework of a stock market includes the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) in India, which regulates and oversees the functioning of the stock market to protect the interests of investors.

concepts.title: Key Players in a Stock Market
concepts.description: Key players in a stock market include brokers, dealers, investors, issuers, and regulators. Brokers act as intermediaries between buyers and sellers, while dealers facilitate the buying and selling of securities. Investors buy and sell securities to earn returns, issuers raise capital by issuing new securities, and regulators oversee the functioning of the stock market to protect investor interests.

concepts.title: Functions of a Depository
concepts.description: A depository provides a safe and efficient way of holding securities in electronic form, eliminating the need for physical certificates. It facilitates seamless transfer of securities and reduces the risk of loss or theft.
